$comment: D-LANG-VOIDPTR-FN-CONVERT (C 6.3.2.3): a BARE function DESIGNATOR (a function name, no `&`) converted IMPLICITLY to a `void*` — the gcc/POSIX dlsym / Tcl ClientData idiom. sqlite test_md5.c hits it 4x: `Tcl_CreateCommand(interp, "md5", MD5DigestToBase16, ...)` passes the bare function as a `void*` ClientData argument -> S0003 without this conversion. Fn-pointer <-> void* is UB in ISO C but POSIX-required and representation-identical on LP64/LLP64 (same width/bits) -> never a miscompile. Gated on `allowVoidPtrFnConvert` (c-subset opts in; default false = strict), the SINGLE authoritative gate for the whole fn<->void* class (Option B). Three legs: (1) bare designator passed DIRECTLY as a `void*` CALL ARGUMENT (the exact test_md5 Tcl_CreateCommand shape), (2) bare designator INIT into void*, (3) a typed Ptr<FnSig> -> void* (Option-B re-homing); each round-trips through void*, casts back, and is CALLED -> all 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: flip the flag off (or revert the isAssignable FnSig->void* arm) -> the bare `register_and_call(add40, 2)` call-arg fails to compile (S0003) -> the example no longer BUILDS. Pure c-subset -> all 4 targets; pe RUNS on Windows. arm64 under qemu; macho on macos-latest.
